headline: Pritchard lights up Philly as the Celtics push for another deep playoff run
morning_brew: Payton Pritchard absolutely took over in Philadelphia, dropping thirty-two points and hitting six shots from deep to bury the 76ers. Jayson Tatum added thirty points and eleven assists as the C's cruised to a 128-96 win. The team now holds a 3-1 lead in the series and looks every bit like the juggernaut that raised Banner 18. Winning by thirty-two points on the road in the playoffs is exactly the kind of statement we needed to see.

The Bruins, on the other hand, were a complete and total disaster at the Garden. Losing 6-1 in a playoff game is unacceptable, and Marco Sturm was right to call it embarrassing. Jeremy Swayman got pulled in the third period after giving up six goals on twenty-nine shots, and the vibe in the building was toxic. This team is now facing elimination, and unless they find some heart fast, this season is ending in a hurry.

Alex Cora is out, along with his coaching staff. An 11-17 record was the breaking point for ownership. I respect the run, particularly that 2018 championship, but this team needed a jolt and the front office finally pulled the trigger. Hope it works. The club responded with a 5-3 win on the road against the Orioles, led by another Willson Contreras home run and six solid innings from the starter named Early.

Round 1, pick 28: Caleb Lomu, OT out of Utah. The offensive line needed a major anchor to protect the pocket. Love it, glaring need addressed. Round 2, pick 55: Gabe Jacas, EDGE out of Illinois. Adding speed to the pass rush is exactly what this defense requires. About time, fills a need. Round 3, pick 95: Eli Raridon, TE out of Notre Dame. We needed a reliable target in the middle of the field. Love it, fills a gap.

Round 5, pick 171: Karon Prunty, CB out of Wake Forest, adds some necessary depth at corner. Round 6, pick 196: Dametrious Crownover, OT out of Texas A&M, more size for the offensive line. Round 6, pick 212: Namdi Obiazor, LB out of TCU, a physical presence for the depth chart. Round 7, pick 234: Behren Morton, QB out of Texas Tech, a developmental arm for the room. Round 7, pick 245: Jam Miller, RB out of Alabama, competition for the backfield rotation. Round 7, pick 247: Quintayvious Hutchins, EDGE out of Boston College, keeping the local kid in town for the defense.

trend_watch.trend: Scored 32 points with 6 three-pointers in a massive playoff win.
trend_watch.dans_take: The kid was unconscious in Philly and showed exactly why he is a vital piece of this rotation.

trend_watch.trend: Allowed 6 goals on 29 shots before getting pulled in a blowout loss.
trend_watch.dans_take: Absolutely brutal night between the pipes when the team needed him most.

trend_watch.trend: Homered for the second straight game in the win over Baltimore.
trend_watch.dans_take: He is bringing some much needed pop to the middle of the order right now.

news_digest.headline: Celtics spoil Embiid's return to action after appendectomy, beat 76ers to take 3-1 series lead
news_digest.dans_take: Great to see the C's take care of business on the road and put the Sixers on the brink.

news_digest.headline: Bruins lose to Sabres in blowout: 'We should all be embarrassed'
news_digest.dans_take: An absolutely pathetic showing when the season is on the line at home.

news_digest.headline: Contreras homers, Red Sox respond to Cora firing with 5-3 win over Orioles
news_digest.dans_take: Good to see them show some life on the road after a massive organizational shakeup.

news_digest.headline: Patriots' NFL Draft Recap
news_digest.dans_take: Lots of new faces to track as the rebuild continues under the new regime.
